title: Ky Fan MAXIMUM principle invoked in Step 4 but only the SUBADDITIVITY theorem is cited

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
In the $\Pi_S$ piece of Step 4 the proof concludes
  Tr(Pi_S M^p Pi_S) <= Tr_sigma(M^p)
and attributes it to 'the Ky Fan max principle', writing
  Tr(Pi_S M^p Pi_S) = Tr(P_{im Pi_S} M^p P_{im Pi_S}) <= Tr_{rank(Pi_S)}(M^p) <= Tr_sigma(M^p).

This step is mathematically TRUE (it is the Ky Fan variational/maximum principle: for symmetric X and a rank-r orthogonal projection P, Tr(PXP) <= sum of the r largest eigenvalues of X = Tr_r(X); and Tr_r(M^p) <= Tr_sigma(M^p) for PSD M^p when r <= sigma). I verified it numerically.

GAP: the document's ONLY citation is [HJ12, Thm. 4.3.47a], which is the Ky Fan SUBADDITIVITY inequality Tr_sigma(X+Y) <= Tr_sigma(X)+Tr_sigma(Y), a DIFFERENT theorem. The maximum principle is invoked here (and is logically essential to the bound) but is never cited or proved. A reader cannot locate the justification, and the two distinct Ky Fan facts are conflated under one reference.

FIX (low difficulty): cite the Ky Fan maximum principle explicitly and separately, e.g. Bhatia, 'Matrix Analysis', Cor. III.4.4 / Problem III.6.5, or Horn-Johnson 'Matrix Analysis' 2nd ed. the Fan maximum-principle statement (around Cor. 4.3.18 / Thm. 4.3.45-46), reserving [HJ12 4.3.47a] for the subadditivity uses (eq. split, Lemma 8). Alternatively include the one-line proof: for P a rank-r projection and X = sum_i mu_i e_i e_i^T (mu_1>=...) with the spectral theorem, Tr(PXP) = sum_i mu_i ||P e_i||^2 with 0<=||P e_i||^2<=1 and sum ||P e_i||^2 = r, hence Tr(PXP) <= sum_{i<=r} mu_i = Tr_r(X).
